diagrams.title: SQL Command Categories
diagrams.description: Shows how DML, DDL, DCL, and TCL commands are categorized including additional features like views, triggers, procedures
diagrams.purpose: Understand the organization of SQL commands and where additional features fit

diagrams.title: Trigger Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Visual representation of BEFORE, AFTER, and INSTEAD OF trigger execution timing relative to DML operations
diagrams.purpose: Understand the sequence of trigger execution and when each type fires

diagrams.title: View Classification
diagrams.description: Classification tree showing types of views - simple vs complex, updatable vs read-only
elements: Simple View (single table, no aggregates) - Always Updatable
Complex View (multi-table or aggregates) - May or May Not Be Updatable
Read-Only View - With READ ONLY constraint
diagrams.purpose: Differentiate view types and their update capabilities

diagrams.title: Transaction State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the states a transaction goes through and where COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and SAVEPOINT operations occur
diagrams.purpose: Visualize transaction flow and understand control points
